channel.title: Texas Monthly: Longreads
channel.description: Публикации из раздела Longreads на Texas Monthly

item.title: The Longhorn’s Long Journey to Becoming a Texas Icon
item.description: How did this ungainly creature develop into a Texas symbol? And what is its role in the twenty-first century? Photographer Joel Salcido traveled thousands of miles—including across the Atlantic—to find out.

item.title: How Johnny Canales Shaped the Rise of Música Mexicana
item.description: A vibrant musical movement is taking over the world. The late tejano icon is its unsung hero.

item.title: The Most Polarizing Thing on Wheels
item.description: A year after its debut, the Cybertruck has come to embody Tesla’s infamous CEO, with critics suggesting it’s as defective and unreliable as Elon Musk himself.

item.title: Is This Texas Oil Icon a Price-Fixing Saudi Collaborator or a Political Scapegoat?
item.description: Scott Sheffield, the former CEO of Pioneer Natural Resources, helped reverse America’s fading energy fortunes. But the feds have accused him of padding his pockets by working with the powerful OPEC cartel.

item.title: Will This 98-Year-Old Sculptor Ever Find a Home for His Gigantic Concrete Presidents?
item.description: David Adickes hopes to literally cement his artistic legacy by installing 43 massive busts somewhere in Texas. But he’s running out of time.

item.title: The Panhandle Is Burning. Can Ranching Survive?
item.description: Wildfires are raging in the region with increasing frequency and intensity. After last year’s historic inferno, Adam and Aubrie Isaacs are among the many ranchers weighing how—and whether—to carry on.

item.title: The Men Spending $1,000 a Day in Pursuit of Big Bass
item.description: Meet the weekend warriors stalking double-digit lunkers on the state’s many private lakes—which themselves cost landowners small fortunes.

item.title: A Deadly Passage
item.description: It's been two years since the worst immigration-related disaster in American history, when 53 migrants were killed in San Antonio after being trapped in a sweltering tractor trailer. We spoke with the families of the victims about justice and, for some, the persistent desire to cross into the United States.

item.title: Un Pasaje a la Muerte
item.description: Han pasado dos años desde el peor desastre inmigratorio en la historia de Estados Unidos, cuando 53 migrantes murieron de calor después de quedar atrapados en un tráiler en San Antonio. Hablamos con las familias de las víctimas sobre la justicia y, para algunos, el persistente deseo de cruzar la

item.title: Chip Roy Doesn’t Care What Anyone Thinks. Not Even Donald Trump.
item.description: The Texas congressman helped launch Ted Cruz’s career. Unlike his former boss, he is unafraid to criticize Trump—which might sideline him.

item.title: Nearly Forgotten, a 1969 Double Murder in Austin Still Haunts Some
item.description: In 1969, two University of Texas students who seemed destined for great things were inexplicably killed. Today their loved ones are still haunted and grieving.

item.title: The King of X
item.description: As Texas led a global revolution in designer party drugs, one restless club kid built an empire of ecstasy. His life is an all-American story of entrepreneurship, moral flexibility, and the heedless pursuit of happiness.

item.title: The Hell We Raised: How Texas Shaped the Gunfighter Era
item.description: Texans left an enduring mark on the gunfighter era. The frontier was a darker place because of it.

item.title: The Final Flight of Captain Forrester
item.description: Fifty years after the last American helicopter departed Saigon, 1,572 Americans are still lost in Vietnam. This is the story of a missing Marine from Odessa, Texas, and how he was found, just in time.

item.title: At 98, the Grandmother of Juneteenth Still Has Work to Do
item.description: “When he calls me, I don’t plan to be in no rocking chair waiting for him," Opal Lee says. "Too much to be done.”

item.title: Why We’re Blowing Up Our 52-Year Tradition of Naming Best and Worst Texas Lawmakers
item.description: Since our founding, we’ve wrapped each legislative session with two lists. In 2025, we decided we couldn't anymore.

item.title: How a Vegas Billionaire’s Plan to Bring Casinos to Texas Went Bust
item.description: The story of Miriam Adelson’s failed bet to legalize gambling—and the right-wing activists and megadonors who stood in her way.

item.title: It Was Already One of Texas’s Strangest Cold Cases. Then a Secretive Figure Appeared.
item.description: Jason Landry’s disappearance confounded the state’s top investigators. When thousands of online sleuths got involved, intrigue turned into obsession.

item.title: “The River House Broke. We Rushed in the River.”
item.description: The July 4 Texas flooding ripped our Kerr County home from its pillars, pulling us into the water and into the night. Then morning came.

item.title: A Young Magician Has Been Secretly Perfecting His Act for Years. Now He’s Ready to Make History.
item.description: Eric Chien plans to unveil a new form of magic at an elite global competition in Italy.
